metadata.seed: Well, the doctors have said that I have cancer again. Had it a couple of years ago, and I beat it. And now they're saying that it's come back. It's not something I really want to talk about, but I'm still working and just trying to get through the day.

patient.query: Well, the doctors have said that I have cancer again. Had it a couple of years ago, and I beat it. And now they're saying that it's come back. It's not something I really want to talk about, but I'm still working and just trying to get through the day.

triggers: doctors saying cancer has come back
automatic_thoughts: I can't believe this is happening again
I have to keep working despite this
behaviors: trying to get through the day

llm_response.response: That must be incredibly difficult to hear, especially after beating cancer before. It sounds like you're holding a lot in while still trying to manage your daily responsibilities. Can you tell me more about what you're feeling right now?

patient.query: I'm just feeling really anxious about my health lately. It’s like there’s this constant worry in the back of my mind that something's wrong, and I can't shake it off. Sometimes I try to distract myself, but then I just end up googling symptoms, which only makes things worse.

entailment: Anxiety about body function or health (finding)
neutral: Anxiety about having a heart attack (finding)

llm_response.response: I hear you're carrying a lot of worry about your health right now. That constant sense of something being wrong sounds incredibly stressful. Can you tell me more about how those thoughts feel to you?

patient.query: Yeah, it’s like a cloud that just hangs over me. I feel this tightness in my chest and sometimes it makes it hard to focus on anything else. Even when I'm with friends or trying to enjoy myself, I can’t help but think about it. It's exhausting, you know?

automatic_thoughts: I can't help but think about it

neutral: Anxiety about having a heart attack (finding)
Anxiety about body function or health (finding)

llm_response.response: That tightness in your chest sounds really uncomfortable, especially when you're trying to relax or spend time with others. It can be draining to feel that way constantly, even when you're surrounded by people you enjoy being with.

patient.query: It really is. I just wish I could enjoy those moments without that nagging voice in my head. It feels like I’m missing out on life because I’m so consumed by this fear. Sometimes I wonder if I'm overreacting, but it doesn’t feel that way in the moment.

neutral: Fear of being left alone during period of dependence (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like that internal voice is really getting in the way of your enjoyment. Can you tell me more about where that voice comes from or what it feels like in your body?

patient.query: It’s hard to pinpoint where it comes from. I guess I've always been a bit of a worrier, but lately, it feels like it has a life of its own. That tightness in my chest turns into this racing heart, and it just spirals from there. It feels suffocating, like there's this weight pressing down on me.

automatic_thoughts: I've always been a bit of a worrier
it feels like it has a life of its own
there's this weight pressing down on me

neutral: Anxiety about having a heart attack (finding)
Fear of having a heart attack (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like the anxiety is taking on a life of its own, creating a cycle that feels overwhelming. Can you tell me more about how this anxiety tends to behave—does it follow any patterns or have certain triggers?

patient.query: Yeah, it definitely seems to have its own routine. Sometimes it kicks up when I hear about someone getting sick or reading a news story about health issues. Other times, it can just hit me out of nowhere, like I'll be going about my day, and then suddenly, I’m consumed with worry. It can feel really random, and that unpredictability makes it worse.

triggers: reading a news story about health issues

llm_response.response: It sounds like this anxiety has a mind of its own, showing up when you least expect it. When you mentioned it feels random, I wonder if there's more to that than just chance?

patient.query: I don’t know, maybe. It’s like sometimes it feels related to something specific, and other times it just feels like it creeps up on me. I just wish I could figure out what triggers it so I could avoid those situations or feelings. It’s frustrating because it feels so out of my control, and I hate that.

automatic_thoughts: I wish I could figure out what triggers it
It feels out of my control

llm_response.response: It sounds really frustrating to feel like you're at the mercy of these emotions, especially when you're trying to understand what's driving them. Maybe there's something you've noticed in the past that might have been different from the times it just seemed to come out of nowhere?

patient.query: I guess there are times when I feel more stressed or overwhelmed in general, like when work gets really busy or if there are personal issues going on. Those times definitely make it worse. But even when things seem okay, the anxiety can still pop up. It's confusing, like I should be able to handle it better, but I just... don't know how.

automatic_thoughts: I should be able to handle it better
the anxiety can still pop up

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're noticing how stress tends to show up in certain situations, even when things aren't obviously overwhelming. When you mentioned feeling like you "should" be able to handle it better, I wonder if that thought feels like it adds pressure to an already difficult experience?

patient.query: Yeah, it really does. That "should" just feels heavy, like I’m putting this expectation on myself to be strong and not let it get to me. But when it does, I end up feeling like I'm failing or something, which just spirals me deeper into the worry. I can’t help but think that maybe I’m not doing enough to manage it, you know?

automatic_thoughts: I'm not doing enough to manage it

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like the pressure you're feeling from these expectations is really weighing on you, and it's understandable how that could make you feel like you're falling short. It's common to feel stuck in that cycle of worry when we're trying to meet our own high standards.

patient.query: Yeah, it really does feel heavy. I want to be strong, and I want to manage my fears, but instead, it feels like I'm just going in circles. I find myself saying, “Why can’t I just get over this?” And that just adds to the frustration. It’s like I’m on one side of a wall, and I can see everyone else on the other side living their lives, while I’m just stuck here.

automatic_thoughts: Why can’t I just get over this?

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)
neutral: Working through the pain of grief (finding)

llm_response.response: I hear how frustrating it must be to feel stuck in this cycle, especially when you're trying to be strong. Can you tell me more about what it feels like when you say, "Why can't I just get over this?" What might be different about this situation if you didn't feel the pressure to be strong right now?
